execution_receipt_contract.purpose: Task Ledger execution receipts preserve the proof that claimed work reached an evidence-backed closeout state.
execution_receipt_contract.commit_hash_rule: A commit_hash is required for landed git closeout receipts. It may be absent only for explicitly validated-uncommitted closeouts where the system could not write git metadata.
execution_receipt_contract.validated_uncommitted_rule: Validated-uncommitted receipts do not replace scoped commits. They make the blocked commit state auditable by carrying validation refs, a no-commit reason, and optional commit_blocker_refs until a later landed receipt can supersede the exceptional closeout.

parent_cas_churn_receipt_boundary.rule: When scoped landing loses the parent compare-and-swap because HEAD advances after a fresh preflight or validation pass, rerun overlap checks, preflight, and focused validation once on the new parent. If the refreshed retry also loses the parent race, stop the commit loop and record a validated-uncommitted execution receipt instead of inventing a new closeout state or repeating blind commits.
parent_cas_churn_receipt_boundary.artifact_receipt_rule: If a generated or untracked proof file is cited as validation evidence, the validated-uncommitted payload must carry its path, role, and whether re-entry should commit it, regenerate it, or supersede it. Chat prose is not a substitute for this artifact ref.
non_goals: Does not replace a scoped commit when the parent is stable.
Does not allow arbitrary commitless closeout states.
Does not treat disjoint parent churn as a same-path blocker.
Does not authorize broad checkpoint or generated projection landing without separate ownership.

execution_receipt_contract.authority_mutation_checkpoint_rule: A clean Task Ledger execution-receipt closeout must not append into pre-existing dirty Task Ledger authority/projection state without an anchor decision. The apply lane must either classify a clean pre-append authority baseline, checkpoint/isolate the authority delta through the governed state lane, refuse before mutation, or residualize the unanchored mutation with a re-entry condition.
execution_receipt_contract.terminal_execution_receipt_dependency_ru: For dependency and action-queue projections, exact terminal receipt states and owner-namespaced states ending in _landed count as terminal execution receipts when the WorkItem is not retired. This does not expand validated-uncommitted receipt closeout states or remove commit_hash requirements for landed git closeouts.

validation_hardening.strict_json_rule: Task Ledger validation MUST parse WorkItem-owned JSON and JSONL with duplicate-key rejection. Standard json.loads success is insufficient because duplicate keys silently keep the last value.
strict_json_surfaces: obsidian/okay lets do this/09 - Raw-Seed Preservation, Semantic Reset, and Fresh Execution Spine/raw_seed/raw_seed_principles.json
validation_hardening.parser_rule: Duplicate top-level and nested object keys reject the surface before schema or projection validation.
validation_hardening.jsonl_rule: Every non-empty events.jsonl line is parsed independently with duplicate-key rejection before event schema checks.
validation_hardening.why: Doctrine/state artifacts can otherwise pass parse checks while losing source fields such as updated_at or last_reconciled_at.

state_machine.rule: captured is low-friction; ready requires enough integration discovery; claimed/active require owner or Work Ledger claim evidence; done requires sign-off and propagation or explicit no-propagation receipt.

terminal_attention_states.rule: Done, propagated, and retired WorkItems are terminal for organizer attention queues such as promotion_candidates, execution_menu, and actionable contract-debt diagnostics. Propagated may satisfy downstream dependencies, but it must not remain schedulable, promotable, or counted as unfinished work.

fields.multi_agent_eligibility: Whether parallel agents may work child items.
fields.expected_base_projection: Optional projection hash/cursor observed before append.
fields.work_ledger_refs: References to Work Ledger sessions, claims, path claims, read receipts, runtime status, and work_ledger.jsonl events.

concurrency_contract.no_orphan_mutation_rule: A WorkItem must not be active without owner/session/claim evidence, explicit blocked state, or stale/orphan projection entry.
concurrency_contract.boundary: Do not recreate Work Ledger session/path-claim machinery; reference it.

fields.route: chatgpt_bridge | claude_code | codex | local | daemon | provider_worker
fields.allowed_agent_lanes: type_a | type_b | bridge | provider_worker | operator | daemon
fields.recommended_thinking_level: displayed recommendation; never brittle Chrome control unless verified.
fields.confidence_to_start: Minimum confidence to move from shaping to ready/active.
fields.authority_gate: operator/controller/standard/test gate required before high-blast transitions.

fields.acceptance_checks: Commands, tests, projections, schema checks, review checks.
fields.evidence_required: Commits, receipts, logs, screenshots, provider receipts, bridge receipts.
fields.lessons: What changed in the general system.
fields.propagation_targets: Standards, skills, paper modules, seeds, prompt shelf, HUD/bridge/frontend/provider lanes.

completion_contract.closeout_slice_helper: tools/meta/factory/task_ledger_apply.py closeout-slice records a WorkItem sign-off, rebuilds projections, validates the event log, and prints Work Ledger close/finalize commands so session-finalize remains last.
completion_contract.work_ledger_closeout_order: If a Type A session touched work, append Work Ledger progress/closeout evidence while the read receipt is still live, then run session-finalize. session-finalize releases active claims by default; use --no-release-claims only for diagnostics. Work Ledger writes after session-finalize are invalid by design.

projection_view_model.determinism_rule: Rebuilding projections twice from the same event log must produce the same payload when generated_at is fixed.
projection_view_model.dependency_satisfied: A hard dependency is satisfied when the upstream WorkItem has state done or propagated, has sign-off evidence, has a terminal execution receipt while not retired, or the dependent WorkItem carries an explicit dependency_resolutions entry with status resolved, satisfied, waived, superseded, or folded. Terminal execution receipts include exact terminal closeout states and owner-namespaced closeout states ending in _landed. Retired is not satisfied by default; it requires explicit resolution, rewiring, or waiver.

compression_authoring_contract.purpose: Every task ships its own compressed rungs so option-surface browse can enumerate the ledger before opening individual entries. Pattern inherited from std_skill.json.
compression_authoring_contract.projection_rule: Option surfaces must prefer compression_passport. Tasks lacking atom/flag/card create authoring_debt and surface in the ledger's debt projection.

identity.purpose: Cheapest browse row: select a task by id, slug, status, rank.

rank_card.purpose: Operating decision row: apply, reprioritize, or sign off.

context.purpose: Bounded working packet for actually doing the task.

evidence.purpose: Source-authority packet: open raw_seed paragraphs, commits, paper modules referenced.
minimum_payload: depends_on with current status of each

history.purpose: Audit trail: rank_history, notes, sign_off lookup, lessons_propagated chain.

telescope_facets.rule: id, slug, title, status, rank are the cheapest identity facets.
Expand at context/evidence bands; never at identity.
atom expands at identity, flag/card at rank_card, full at context.
Expands at history; tail-only at rank_card.

band:context.source: event-sourced task projection, dependency views, and evidence refs

band:evidence.emitter: task-specific evidence_command or cited source refs
band:evidence.source: state/task_ledger/events.jsonl, commits, paper modules, standards, and raw-seed refs named by the WorkItem

band:history.source: state/task_ledger/events.jsonl replay and projected notes/rank/signoff fields

currentness_policy.rule: Task Ledger option-surface rows are read models over state/task_ledger/events.jsonl; rebuild/check projections before treating rank, dependency, capture, or signoff counts as current.

dependency_neighborhood_policy.rule: Expose hard depends_on, downstream unlock, signoff, and blocking anomaly edges as first-order context; keep broad dependencies, historic notes, and distant campaign clusters as counts with drilldown refs.

naming_contract.id_format: task_NNN, snake_case sequential within ledger. Example: task_001
naming_contract.file_location: state/task_ledger/ledger.json (canonical) and per-phase ledgers at obsidian/.../<phase>/task_ledger.json (optional projection)
naming_contract.registry_entry: When kind-atlas registration lands (v1), this file appears at state/task_ledger/ledger.json with the canonical schema.

promotion_rules.completion_to_sign_off: When a task reaches status='completed', a sign_off row SHOULD be authored in state/task_ledger/sign_offs.json per std_task_sign_off.json. The sign_off_id back-reference closes the loop.
promotion_rules.retired_without_completion: Tasks may be retired without completion when superseded, abandoned, or invalidated by reversal. The retirement justification becomes a final rank_history entry with rank=null.
promotion_rules.ledger_compression: When >100 completed/retired tasks accumulate, the ledger MAY emit a compressed projection at state/task_ledger/ledger_compressed.json grouping by completion period. Original entries remain in ledger.json as authority.
